failures.explanation: Multi-edit strain (2 simultaneous perturbations, type=triple_knockout). Model geometric-mean / Hill composition does not capture combinatorial epistasis in this regime.
Multi-edit strain (2 simultaneous perturbations, type=triple_knockout). Model geometric-mean / Hill composition does not capture combinatorial epistasis in this regime.
Combined perturbation (combined) — multiple simultaneous inputs (e.g. OE + chassis + medium). Framework does not decompose the individual contributions.
failures.fix_strategy: Check each component; consider adding chassis / medium source nodes.

failures.explanation: Multi-edit strain (4 simultaneous perturbations, type=combined). Model geometric-mean / Hill composition does not capture combinatorial epistasis in this regime.

Deletion / KD of RpoS should DECREASE lycopene but model predicts unchanged (ratio=0.968). Cascade from RpoS is either missing, inverted, or RpoS only acts through a path that is buffered by parallel supply (MEP || MVA).
failures.fix_strategy: Check whether RpoS has sufficient downstream weight and whether parallel paths should be weakened in WT.

failures.explanation: Perturbation of FNR (type=knockout) should leave lycopene unchanged, but model predicts increased (ratio=1.066). Likely a composite node that over-weights a redundant member or a spurious cascade edge.
failures.fix_strategy: Review composite membership and single-member modifier for FNR; consider modifier=0.99 for single-member perturbation of a redundant family.

summary.notes: Failures categorised on ODE (best method, 66.3% accuracy). Many 'increased expected, decreased predicted' failures on OE tests suggest cascade-sign or dilution problems downstream of DXS/IDI. A second common mode: chassis/medium/inducer tests where the source node is not wired — these read as unchanged or spurious movement.
